items.title: Show HN: Nutshot Simulator – 31 days, 59K prompts, 142M tokens, ~$23K of Claude
items.why_it_matters: An indie developer shipped a viral simulator game built entirely with Claude, racking up 142M tokens in 31 days and proving how far you can push pure AI generation for a live web app. It is a masterclass in managing large-scale LLM state and token costs for a real product. This week, read their build breakdown to see how they structured their prompts, and apply one of their token-saving state patterns to your own Next.js app.

items.title: Show HN: My design portfolio is a Claude agent (no vector DB, no RAG stack)
items.why_it_matters: A designer shipped a portfolio website that runs entirely as a Claude agent without needing a complex RAG stack or vector database, keeping the architecture dead simple to get the project live. This proves you don't need over-engineered backends to build interactive AI features. This week, try building a simple, single-route 'agent' page in your Next.js app that answers questions about your project using a hardcoded system prompt instead of setting up a database.

items.title: SnippAI – Screenshot, speak, straight into your Claude Code session
items.why_it_matters: SnippAI is a new utility that lets you take a screenshot, speak your instructions, and pipe the asset and transcript directly into your active Claude Code terminal session. It removes the friction of saving, dragging, and typing out UI bugs when working with terminal-based coding agents. This week, download the tool and use it to feed a screenshot of a broken UI element in your Next.js frontend directly to Claude Code to fix it in one command.

items.title: Show HN: Sentinel – open-source QA agent that reads your code before it clicks
items.why_it_matters: Sentinel is an open-source QA agent that reads your codebase and automatically tests your UI flows by simulating user clicks before you deploy. It helps solo developers catch breaking frontend bugs without writing manual, tedious test suites. This week, install Sentinel in your Next.js repository to run a quick automated sweep of your AI-ingest app before your next Vercel deployment.

items.title: Show HN: HeimWall – Catch secrets before you paste them into Claude or Cursor
items.why_it_matters: HeimWall is a local tool that intercepts your clipboard to catch API keys and database secrets before you accidentally paste them into Claude or Cursor. Since you are working with Supabase and Vercel keys daily, this keeps your credentials safe without slowing down your development flow. This week, install the lightweight client to ensure you don't leak your Supabase service role key during your next Claude Code debugging session.

items.title: 1Password for Claude: Give Claude access without giving up your credentials
items.why_it_matters: 1Password launched an official integration for Claude, allowing you to securely share API keys and credentials with the model without hardcoding them or pasting them in plain text. This makes it much safer to build integrations that require external service access. This week, set up the integration so you can safely pass your Supabase or Vercel API keys to Claude when automating deployment tasks.
